steps.canonical_text: If a fall, crash, or strong blow may have hurt the neck or back, do not move them. Support the head in the position you found it.
Check if they respond to your voice and whether they are breathing normally. Treat occasional gasps as not breathing.
If there is a cut on the scalp, apply gentle pressure with a clean cloth. A cold pack wrapped in cloth can ease swelling on a bump.

Call emergency services now if you see any of: loss of consciousness, repeated vomiting, severe headache, confusion, seizure, unequal pupils, clear fluid or blood from ear or nose, or weakness on one side.
Do not give aspirin or other pain medication after a head injury, as some can worsen bleeding.
Keep them still and observe for several hours. Even if they feel fine, get medical evaluation after any significant head impact.
